.engine/operations/build-orchestration.m.why: build-orchestration is the convergence runbook — where every other system's build work is actually carried out (plan, review, integrate, submit, routine, reconcile). The 120-line operations nudge starves the one runbook that legitimately spans all of those, so it carries a raised ceiling: deliberate room to grow as the build process it documents grows, not a tight fit to today's length. The ceiling moved 200 to 215 when the runbook gained the consumed-review-lenses record plus the dry-run disclosure line, and 215 to 250 when issue #414 completed the plan gate against the locked design — adding the fast-path floor and its guardrail/schema exclusion, the proportionate progress-view checklist tier, the three-input coverage proposal (what review is installed, the operator's remembered depth preference, and what sits next to the work), the advise-re-audit rider, the Plan-time decomposability recommendation, and the fifth step-rendering rule (a step must be able to fail). All of it is load-bearing build-process content that would otherwise force trimming a standing operator-disclosure paragraph to fit. The raise lives in this guarded rule, so changing it needs the operator's deliberate sign-off — which is what issue #273 asked for: a recorded reason, rather than a runbook silently sitting over budget.

message: An operation (a runbook under .engine/operations/) must contain, as level-2 headings in this order, Purpose, then Steps, then Done when, and it may also contain a Notes section, and no others. These sections are what make a runbook followable — what it is for, the ordered steps, and how to know it is done — so an operation that omits a required one is structurally incomplete. This check catches a broken SHAPE only: that the required sections are present, correctly ordered, and that no stray section appears. It cannot judge whether a section's content is substantive — an empty or hand-wavy section passes here and is caught instead by the operator reviewing the committed change, and whether the runbook actually tells a cold operator how to perform the procedure is the audits layer's judgment, not this check's. Length over an operation's line budget is only a soft nudge to trim, never a block; the budget is 120 by default, and a specific operation may carry a higher recorded budget in length_budget_overrides. A malformed or stale override entry — one missing its budget or recorded reason, or naming no operation — is the rule's hard tier, so a dead budget grant cannot accumulate. Add or reorder the flagged sections to match the operation template (.engine/templates/operation.md) before merging.
